rights.basis: The underlying text is a fifteenth-century work; this assessment is separate from any modern edition, encoding, photograph, or contractual access condition.

access.accessMethod: No official public complete digital facsimile was verified in current catalog and discovery searches; a 1979 printed facsimile exists separately.

Use Cambridge's ordering-images route. Ask first for a digital reproduction of the complete physical manuscript—covers/boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto and verso, and any separately imaged edges—and separately identify the poem at modern fols. 53r-54r. Request confirmation of omissions, resolution, color, delivery format, price, and publication/redistribution terms.
notes: The published minimum charge is £25. Supplied copies are for research/private use by default; reproduction or online publication requires written permission.

rights.basis: Cambridge's supply terms limit normal orders to research/private use and require written permission for reproduction, including online publication.
rights.component: Any newly supplied Cambridge manuscript photography

entities.description: Fischer's C and base witness for alpha. Fischer gives fols. 45r-46r; the modern foliation places the poem at fols. 53r-54r.
entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38

rights.basis: Cambridge's supply terms limit normal orders to research/private use and require written permission for reproduction, including online publication.
rights.component: Any newly supplied Cambridge manuscript photography

access.accessMethod: The exact current catalog record exposes no Digitised Content link; this is dated search evidence, not proof that no internal or later digitization exists.

Use the catalog's Reference Services/contact route to ask whether a complete digital reproduction can be supplied. Define the requested physical coverage (boards/covers, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, edges if separately imaged), then separately identify the poem at fols. 297r-300v and ask about price, format, omissions, and publication rights.

entities.description: Fischer's H2, collated for alpha. The poem is at fols. 297r-300v within a poetry unit dated about 1456.
entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 5396

access.accessMethod: No auditable official complete public facsimile was verified. The current archive catalog endpoint was inaccessible during review and the LUNA interface presented an access-verification barrier; neither was bypassed.

Use the online imaging-services order form. Ask first for a digital reproduction of the complete physical manuscript—covers/boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every page/leaf, and any separately imaged edges—and separately identify the poem at pp. 186-192. Request confirmation of omissions, resolution, color, delivery format, total price, and publication/redistribution terms.
notes: The posted price list gives £14.40 per high-resolution image; standard PDFs are £8.40 for the first image and £1.20 for each additional image, with a 40-image order limit. Permission to reproduce is separate and supplied rights are nontransferable.

rights.basis: Lambeth's imaging page separates supply from permission to reproduce and states that supplied rights are nontransferable.
rights.component: Any newly supplied Lambeth manuscript photography

entities.description: Fischer's L and base witness for beta. The poem occupies manuscript pp. 186-192 in the modern METS locator; Fischer gives pp. 186-193.

holding.notes: The catalog homepage is supplied because an auditable exact current public item page could not be retrieved.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853

rights.basis: Lambeth's imaging page separates supply from permission to reproduce and states that supplied rights are nontransferable.
rights.component: Any newly supplied Lambeth manuscript photography

entities.description: Fischer's B, collated for beta. The poem begins on image page 335 and ends on page 337, corresponding to fols. 157r-158r; Stans puer ad mensam begins on page 338/f. 158v.
entities.label: Balliol College MS 354

notes: Associated with the London grocer Richard Hill.

access.accessMethod: The exact current catalog record exposes no Digitised Content link; this is dated search evidence, not proof that no internal or later digitization exists.

Use the catalog's Reference Services/contact route to ask whether a complete digital reproduction can be supplied. Define the requested physical coverage (boards/covers, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, edges if separately imaged), then separately identify the poem at fols. 61r-63v and ask about price, format, omissions, and publication rights.

rights.basis: No item-specific reproduction license was verified. Any quotation, publication, or repository redistribution must follow the terms supplied with an order or written response.
rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library manuscript photography for Harley 2399 or Harley 5396

dateStatements.label: manuscript part
dateStatements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

entities.description: Fischer's incomplete H1 and principal base for gamma. The poem occupies fols. 61r-63v in a Bodmin/Cornwall production unit, perhaps written by John Arcuarius.
entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2399

notes: The relevant production unit is associated with Bodmin, Cornwall.

rights.basis: No item-specific reproduction license was verified. Any quotation, publication, or repository redistribution must follow the terms supplied with an order or written response.
rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library manuscript photography for Harley 2399 or Harley 5396

entities.description: Fischer's A branch, used indirectly through Furnivall's transcription. The poem is item 3 at fol. 6r-v; it omits stanzas 6-11, the first half of stanza 13, and stanza 14 in the catalog's reckoning.
entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 61

notes: Made in England, perhaps Leicester; copied by the scribe who signs as Rate or Rathe.
No significant decoration or rubrication; yellow wash is used selectively. Rebound in 1986.

entities.description: The modern working transcript from which Fischer printed alpha; it is not the medieval codex.

next_steps: Use the recorded Cambridge image-order route and request the whole physical manuscript before naming fols. 53r-54r as the work portion.

Ask for covers/boards, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, and any separately imaged edges; request an omissions list, resolution, color profile, file format, price, and delivery method.
Ask separately whether the delivered files or derived reference crops may be placed in a public Git repository or used in profit-oriented print-on-demand research.
openQuestions.question: Can Cambridge supply a complete color digital reproduction of CUL MS Ff.2.38, and under what private-use, repository-publication, and commercial-publication terms?

next_steps: Submit separate reference enquiries for both shelfmarks, defining complete physical coverage before identifying the work portions at Harley 5396 fols. 297r-300v and Harley 2399 fols. 61r-63v.
Ask whether existing photography already covers the whole objects; if not, request a quotation and an explicit list of omitted boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, leaves, or edges.
Ask for written repository and commercial-publication terms before caching or redistributing any supplied files.
openQuestions.question: Can the British Library supply complete digital reproductions of Harley MS 5396 and Harley MS 2399, and what reuse terms would attach?

next_steps: Use the imaging-services form to ask whether a multi-order or custom whole-manuscript project is possible, defining complete physical coverage first and pp. 186-192 second.
Ask whether a pre-existing complete microfilm or digital surrogate can be supplied more economically than per-image photography.
Obtain separate written permission terms for public Git hosting, scholarly quotation, and commercial print-on-demand research before redistributing supplied images.
openQuestions.question: Can Lambeth Palace Library supply a complete digital reproduction of MS 853 despite the posted 40-image order limit, and what would the total cost and reuse terms be?

next_steps: Search Varnhagen archival papers, Fischer correspondence, and Erlangen editorial archives for transcripts of Ff.2.38, Harley 5396, Lambeth 853, Balliol 354, and Harley 2399.
If found, catalog each transcript's shelfmark, completeness, date, and deviations from both codex and Fischer.
openQuestions.question: Do Hermann Varnhagen's five working transcripts survive, and can their readings be checked against the codices?

next_steps: Consult the 1979 facsimile introduction and the current CUL catalog when accessible.
Record any flyleaf or inserted-leaf counting rule that explains the eight-folio offset rather than silently replacing the historical citation.
openQuestions.question: What exact physical change accounts for Fischer's fols. 45r-46r and the modern fols. 53r-54r in CUL MS Ff.2.38?

next_steps: Compare complete diplomatic transcriptions or facsimiles of all six witnesses rather than inferring a stemma from Fischer's edited groupings.
Review recent textual scholarship for a defensible relationship model and retain uncertainty where dependence or contamination cannot be established.
openQuestions.question: How are the six manuscript witnesses and Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma related beyond Fischer's unresolved grouping?

reviewNotes: Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma are editorial groupings, not three independent works and not three extant codices.

Working transcripts are typed manuscript_witness because schema version 1.0.0 has no intermediary-transcript entity type; their labels, dates, survival status, and relation chains distinguish them from medieval witnesses.

Complete facsimile coverage is always relative to the provider's exposed IIIF inventory. The Balliol object lacks some physical exteriors and edges even though all 528 exposed canvases were cached; the Ashmole object contains all 358 exposed canvases.
A private, gitignored cache demonstrates download status but does not grant redistribution or publication rights.

lineage.summary: CME00042 is the General Corpus copy of a University of Michigan electronic text derived from Rudolf Fischer's 1889 How the Wyse Man Taught Hys Sone: In drei Texten. The electronic selection contains Fischer's two title pages and printed pp. 27-49: alpha, based on Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 and collated with Harley MS 5396; beta, based on Lambeth Palace Library MS 853 and collated with Balliol College MS 354; and gamma, based chiefly on Harley MS 2399 but supplemented through Frederick Furnivall's 1869 transcription of Bodleian MS Ashmole 61. Fischer worked from Hermann Varnhagen's transcripts for the first five witnesses rather than directly from those codices. Complete provider-exposed image sequences for Balliol MS 354 and Ashmole MS 61 have been cached privately, and each cache maps the poem's leaves inside the whole manuscript. No verified public complete facsimile was found for Ff.2.38, Harley 5396, Lambeth 853, or Harley 2399; request routes are recorded without converting those dated search results into claims that the manuscripts have never been digitized.

catalogSummary.title: How the Wise Man Taught His Son

catalogingSubject.label: How the Wise Man Taught His Son: texts alpha, beta, and gamma
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the U-M/General Corpus electronic selection from Rudolf Fischer's 1889 edition: two edition title pages and Fischer's complete three edited version-groups on printed pp. 27-49. It is one anonymous Middle English advice poem represented through three reconstructed textual groupings, not three independent works, not one homogeneous critical text, and not a direct transcription of any single manuscript. Fischer's introduction, notes, and index are outside the electronic unit.

dateStatements.display: Circa 1430 in Lambeth Palace Library MS 853

scope.description: Earliest conventionally dated witness among the six currently identified

extent.description: The XML body has three DIV2 version containers. Their base-text hierarchy contains 24, 19, and 20 LG stanzas and 192, 152, and 160 L elements, totaling 63 stanzas and 504 verse lines. The file also contains 436 NOTE elements, 100 line-number milestones, 25 PB elements, and two title-page DIV1 elements. These are representation metrics for Fischer's three editorial texts, not a single canonical work-line count or a claim that every witness has 504 lines.

formStatements.description: The work is an eight-line-stanza advice poem. Fischer's three texts have 24, 19, and 20 stanzas respectively in the XML hierarchy.

scope.description: All three edited medieval version texts and their manuscript variants
Fischer's headings and apparatus retained in the electronic selection

notes: Metadata describes the anonymous work and the CME edited selection separately from individual manuscripts, Fischer's edition, and modern supporting editions.
The complete Ashmole and Balliol facsimiles are recorded in the linked lineage manifest; this metadata file does not infer work-level geography or formatting from those two witnesses.
The DIMEV/NIMEV identifiers apply to the textual work/tradition; individual witness incipits and local titles remain separately recorded.

subjects.label: A father's moral and practical instruction to his son
Marriage, household governance, and treatment of dependants
Speech, truth, reputation, and civic responsibility

summaries.value: An anonymous Middle English father-to-son advice poem in which a wise man teaches a fifteen-year-old heir how to govern speech, work, worship, money, office-holding, marriage, servants, food and drink, tavern-going, gambling, charity, and preparation for death. Its practical household counsel is framed by Christian piety and the instability of worldly wealth.

CME00042 presents Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma texts with exhaustive paired-witness footnotes. Alpha is based on Cambridge Ff.2.38 with Harley 5396 variants; beta on Lambeth 853 with Balliol 354 variants; gamma chiefly on Harley 2399, with Ashmole 61 readings and three continuous stanzas supplied from the Ashmole branch.

The six witnesses differ in date, place, wording, completeness, and stanza inventory. The production place or dialect of one codex must not be assigned to the work as a whole. Fischer's Greek-letter texts are editorial groupings and gamma is explicitly composite rather than a surviving manuscript text.

assertion.notes: A normalized conventional title. Manuscript wording and spelling vary, and the Cambridge witness uniquely uses 'goode man' in its heading.
